manifest.title: KefTrade Phase 9.12 Elite Candidate Report
manifest.description: Technical audit of the volatility-adaptive relative-strength campaign and elite promotion result.

metric_definitions: Strict promotions are campaign jobs that passed the unchanged single-market evidence rules.
Elite candidates pass the unchanged aggregate cross-validation function.
Operational failures count jobs with status failed, not evidence rejections.
query.description: Campaign-level counts reconciled across jobs, elite candidates, and candidate-linked simulation deployments.
query.sql: SELECT c.id, c.requested_candidates, c.completed_jobs, c.failed_jobs, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE j.status = 'promoted') AS strict_promotions, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE j.status = 'rejected') AS evidence_rejections, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM elite_research_candidates e WHERE e.campaign_id = c.id AND e.simulation_only = TRUE) AS elite_candidates, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM strategy_deployments d WHERE d.campaign_id = c.id AND d.candidate_id IS NOT NULL AND d.simulation_only = TRUE) AS candidate_linked_deployments FROM research_campaigns c JOIN research_campaign_jobs j ON j.campaign_id = c.id WHERE c.id = 7 GROUP BY c.id;

metric_definitions: Profit factor is aggregate gross profit divided by aggregate gross loss across campaign markets.
Stability is the fraction of candidate market jobs that passed every strict single-market rule.
Assets passed counts distinct assets with strict job promotion.
query.description: Persisted elite summaries ordered by research score.
query.sql: SELECT candidate_id, profit_factor, expectancy, max_drawdown, trade_count, stability, assets_passed, timeframes_passed, regimes_passed FROM elite_research_candidates WHERE campaign_id = 7 AND simulation_only = TRUE ORDER BY research_score DESC;

query.description: AAPL and NVDA strict validation metrics for the highest-ranked elite candidate.
query.sql: SELECT symbol, status, result->'metrics'->>'profit_factor' AS profit_factor, result->'metrics'->>'expectancy_per_trade' AS expectancy, result->'metrics'->>'number_of_trades' AS trades, result->'metrics'->>'max_drawdown' AS drawdown FROM research_campaign_jobs WHERE campaign_id = 7 AND candidate_id = 'sd_fd2d45d9ec1dc9' ORDER BY symbol;

tables.title: Best candidate by market
tables.subtitle: AAPL and NVDA 1h out-of-sample validation; both strict jobs promoted

tables.title: Elite candidate audit table
tables.subtitle: Exact persisted aggregate metrics for all Phase 9.12 elite candidates

blocks.body:
## Result

Phase 9.12 produced **6 elite candidates** under unchanged gates: **24 jobs completed**, **17 strict promotions**, **7 evidence rejections**, and **0 operational failures**. Six candidate-linked deployments are simulation-only. Phase 10 was not started; eligible forward trades remain zero.

## Method and Limits

The **Volatility-Adaptive Relative Strength** family uses one asset-agnostic rule set with observed-volatility profiles and benchmark-relative continuation. The backtester now honors decision-level reward multiples. Existing PF, expectancy, drawdown, trade-count, walk-forward, regime, stability, asset, and timeframe gates are unchanged.

`sd_fd2d45d9ec1dc9` leads with PF **1.3431**, expectancy **19.6157**, drawdown **0.0499**, and **66 trades**. Five additional elite controls have PF **1.2759-1.3220**, **66-73 trades**, and **1.0 stability**. This is deterministic historical evidence, not independent forward proof: selection used the stored history, transfer covers two assets and one execution timeframe, and multiple-testing risk remains. Keep Phase 10 locked and collect untouched simulation-only forward evidence without retuning.
